title: podman logs produces blank lines
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

`podman logs` does not mirror console output.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create an image that runs wildfly configured with a logger that uses a console handler and a file handler.

2. Run the image in detached mode.

3. Execute `podman logs -t <container-name> | head -n100`

4. Execute `podman exec  <container-name>  head -n100 /opt/wildfly/standalone/log/server.log`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
podman logs -t <container-name>  | head -n100
2020-01-24T12:39:23.808274859+01:00 =========================================================================
2020-01-24T12:39:23.808274859+01:00

**Describe the results you expected:**

The output of the logger should be the same in both versions. There should not be so many blank lines.

For example podman logs after
```
2020-01-24T12:39:24.179597379+01:00 11:39:24,169 INFO  [org.jboss.modules] (main) JBoss Modules version 1.9.0.Final
2020-01-24T12:39:24.450303989+01:00 
2020-01-24T12:39:24.459430281+01:00 
...
``` 
should mirror the log file content:
```
2020-01-24 11:39:24,169 INFO  [org.jboss.modules] (main) JBoss Modules version 1.9.0.Final
2020-01-24 11:39:24,449 INFO  [org.jboss.msc] (main) JBoss MSC version 1.4.5.Final
2020-01-24 11:39:24,459 INFO  [org.jboss.threads] (main) JBoss Threads version 2.3.3.Final
...
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version: 1.7.0
RemoteAPI Version: 1
Go Version: go1.13.5
OS/Arch: linux/amd64
```

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

I'm running (rootless) containers inside a simple podman pod...

comments.body:
Thanks for taking the time to open the issue, @tanwald !

> Create an image that runs wildfly configured with a logger that uses a console handler and a file handler.

Could you provide instructions to do that? A complete reproducer would be great.

@baude, any suspicion?
Is this just logs from `exec`? Can you reproduce this by `head`ing the log file in a `podman run` command?

Also - is the log file syncronized in any way? Are we potentially seeing the Conmons for `podman run` and `podman exec` trampling each other?

Including the log file from a container that does this (you can find the location from `LogPath` in `podman inspect` on the container) would help a lot for debugging.
It seems my concerns are unfounded - looks like `exec` is either not logged at all, or logged to a separate file. Would still appreciate the log file from a container in which this happened, though
reproducer would help.  i mean this is not really that the logs is producing empty lines, it is that the service is and we are following suit.

Hi, you could run
`podman run --rm --name logging -d quay.io/serenditree/branch-seed:latest `
and compare 
`podman exec logging head -n 400 /opt/wildfly/standalone/log/server.log `
vs
`podman logs logging | head -n 400 `
You can inspect the logging configuration by running 
`podman exec logging cat /opt/wildfly/standalone/configuration/standalone.xml | sed -n '/urn:jboss:domain:logging/,/<\/subsystem/p' `
I hope this helps. Thanks for investigating...
The mongodb/kafka exceptions are in this context intentional/predictable - real world example, I thought... Try to debug with podman logs.. ;)
Confirmed, I can reproduce
I think we're seeing control characters in the logs that might be causing this

Yeah, this is definitely terminal control characters in the output.

@haircommander I wonder if we actually want Conmon to log characters like this...
what log driver? file or journald
File
BTW, I believe `podman logs` is only showing the STDOUT/STDERR of pid1 of the container. There is nothing that logs `podman exec`, since this is a random process inside of the container.
@haircommander Any further thoughts on this one?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@haircommander Any thoughts on this, should we check if a character is printable before sending it to the log file?
no thoughts yet. I'll try to get to addressing this soon
@haircommander Would you work with one of the interns on this?
@ParkerVR Please work on this.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@ParkerVR Any movement on this?
This seems to have been fixed in the upstream. @tanwald let us know if this is still occurring for you.
@ParkerVR it works on my machine... thanks!
